arguments.name: fix-non-serialization
arguments.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, apply these AOT-compatibility fixes to non-serialization files. Use the `edit` tool for each change.

## 1. ArmEnvironment.cs (line 80)

Replace:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
With:
```csharp
public override string ToString()
{
    return $"{{\"Endpoint\":\"{Endpoint}\",\"Audience\":\"{Audience}\",\"DefaultScope\":\"{DefaultScope}\"}}";
}
```
This is a simple struct with 3 string/Uri properties. Manual formatting avoids all JsonSerializer usage. (The original just produced `{"Endpoint":"...","Audience":"...","DefaultScope":"..."}`)

## 2. ArmClientOptions.cs (line 52)

Replace:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
```
With:
```csharp
using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
```
Wait, actually the `span` variable is `BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span`. We need to parse it. Let me provide the full method body fix.

Current code (lines 48-52 approximately):
```csharp
var assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly();
using (Stream stream = assembly.GetManifestResourceStream(profile.GetManifestName()))
{
    var span = BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span;
    var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
    var armProfile = allProfile["resource-manager"];
```

Replace the `var span` and `var allProfile` lines with:
```csharp
    using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
    var armProfile = profileDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("resource-manager");
```

And then in the loop that follows, `armProfile` is now a `JsonElement` instead of `Dictionary<string, JsonElement>`. The existing loop code is:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile)
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Key;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Change this to:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile.EnumerateObject())
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Name;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Read the full ArmClientOptions.cs file first to see the complete method before editing.

Also remove the `using System.Text.Json;` import ONLY if it's no longer used after the change (it's likely still needed if other files don't reference it). Actually keep it since `JsonElement` comes from `System.Text.Json`.

## 3. Extensions/ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs (line 30)

The warning is on calling `builder.RegisterClientFactory<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions>(configuration)` which comes from an external interface `IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithConfiguration<TConfiguration>` marked with `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` and `[RequiresDynamicCode]`.

Since this is an external API we can't modify, mark the method with the same attributes. Add:
```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder Source Generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types.")]
```
to the `AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>` method.

Also add the required `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` at the top of the file (it already imports `System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis` - check first, add if missing).

## 4. Four LongRunningOperation files

All four have the identical pattern. The `GetOperationId` method contains:
```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
return lroDetails["id"];
```

Replace with:
```csharp
using var lroDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default));
return lroDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("id").GetString();
```

The four files are:
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperation.cs`
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperation.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs`

Each file needs `using System.Text.Json;` added to its imports if not already present (check first).

## 5. ArmOperation.cs (lines 96-111) - IL2087/IL2090

The `IsResource<T>()` method uses reflection (`typeof(T).GetConstructor(...)` and `Activator.CreateInstance(typeof(T), ...)`). Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<T>() where T : notnull
```
To:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T>() where T : notnull
```

Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` if not present. Check the callers of `IsResource<T>()` in the same file and cascade the annotation if needed.

## 6. GenericOperationSource.cs (lines 37, 40) - IL2087

The class `GenericOperationSource<T>` uses `typeof(T)` with `ModelReaderWriter.Read` and `Activator.CreateInstance`. Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the class's generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<T> : IOperationSource<T>
```
To:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

Check if `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` is already present (it is, per the file header).

After these changes, check if the callers of `GenericOperationSource<T>` need cascaded annotations. The main caller is in `ArmOperation.cs` where `new GenericOperationSource<T>(client, isResource)` is called. The `T` there comes from a method like `Rehydrate<T>` or similar — the annotation on `IsResource<T>` should already cover it, but verify the `T` on the containing method also has the annotation.

Read the full ArmOperation.cs to see the method signatures that call `GenericOperationSource<T>` and `IsResource<T>()`, and add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]` to those method's generic `T` parameter as well.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me dispatch the changes using sub-agents for efficiency. Here's my plan:

**Group 1: Bulk `document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)` replacement** (87 files)
This is the biggest change — simply replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, document.RootElement)` with `document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)` across all files.

**Group 2: Complex serialization files**
- `OperationStatusResult.Serialization.cs` - `Error` (ResponseError), `OperationStatusResult` recursive
- `ResourceGroupExportResult.Serialization.cs` - `Error` (ResponseError)


- `GenericResourceData.Serialization.cs` - `Plan` (ArmPlan), `Identity` (ManagedServiceIdentity), `ExtendedLocation`, `SystemData`
- `PolicyAssignmentData.Serialization.cs` - `ManagedIdentity` (ManagedServiceIdentity), `SystemData`
- `TrackedResourceExtendedData.Serialization.cs` - `ExtendedLocation`, `SystemData`
- `EntityData.Serialization.cs` - `Parent` (SubResource), `SystemData`
- `ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs` - handling identity-related serialization
- `PolicyAssignmentPatch.Serialization.cs` - `Identity` (ManagedServiceIdentity)

Beyond the serialization files, there are several non-serialization files that need attention: `ArmEnvironment.cs` with its `ToString()` method, `ArmClientOptions.cs` handling dictionary deserialization, `ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs` for external API concerns, and a set of LongRunningOperation files along with `ArmOperation.cs` and `GenericOperationSource.cs` that involve `DynamicallyAccessedMembers` attributes on generics. I should also check the remaining serialization files that handle `SystemData` deserialization, including `ManagementGroupData.Serialization.cs`.

Looking at the other serialization files with `SystemData` deserialization, they all follow the same pattern where `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText())` gets replaced with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)`. Now I'm planning to dispatch sub-agents to handle the bulk replacements, starting with the `document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)` pattern using bash or sed. the bulk pattern, then move on to the complex files.

arguments.name: bulk-rootElement-fix
arguments.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, replace all occurrences of `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, document.RootElement)` with `document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)` across all `.cs` files. This is a simple mechanical replacement with no edge cases. Use `find` + `sed` since the pattern contains no generics or special characters.

After the replacement, verify the change was applied by running:
```
grep -rn 'JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, document.RootElement)' --include='*.cs'
```
This should return no results.

Also verify the new pattern exists:
```
grep -rn 'document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)' --include='*.cs' | wc -l
```
This should show a count of files changed (should be around 87+ occurrences).

arguments.name: fix-systemdata-deserialize
arguments.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, replace all occurrences of:
```
JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText())
```
with:
```
SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)
```

across all `.cs` files. Use `find` + `sed` for this. The pattern is very consistent across ~14 files.

After the replacement, verify with:
```
grep -rn 'JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>' --include='*.cs'
```
Should return no results.

Also verify the new pattern:
```
grep -rn 'SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value)' --include='*.cs' | wc -l
```
Should return ~14 results.

arguments.name: fix-complex-serialization
arguments.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, apply these AOT-compatibility fixes to serialization files. All types mentioned below implement `IJsonModel<T>` and have static `DeserializeXxx(JsonElement)` methods. The extension method `WriteObjectValue` is available from `ModelSerializationExtensions`.

## File: Common/Generated/Models/OperationStatusResult.Serialization.cs

1. Line 84: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
   - `Error` is of type `ResponseError` (from Azure.Core). WriteObjectValue handles it via IJsonModel<T> or IUtf8JsonSerializable.

2. Line 173: Replace `array.Add(JsonSerializer.Deserialize<OperationStatusResult>(item.GetRawText()));` with `array.Add(DeserializeOperationStatusResult(item));`
   - `DeserializeOperationStatusResult` is a static method on the same class that takes a `JsonElement`. `item` is already a `JsonElement` from `EnumerateArray()`.

3. Line 184: Replace `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText(), AzureResourceManagerModelFactory.ResponseErrorSerializerOptions);`
   - Actually, for ResponseError, we need a different approach since it's external. Use this instead: read the element via IJsonModel. Replace with:
     ```csharp
     using (var document0 = JsonDocument.Parse(property.Value.GetRawText()))
     {
         var reader0 = new Utf8JsonReader(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(property.Value.GetRawText()));
         error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()));
     }
     ```
   WAIT - Actually, `ModelReaderWriter.Read` without a context also uses reflection. Let me reconsider.
   
   The simplest AOT-safe approach: since `ResponseError` has a `[JsonConverter]` attribute, we can use `JsonSerializer.Deserialize` with explicit `JsonSerializerOptions` that include the converter. But actually the warning is on the `JsonSerializer.Deserialize` API itself.

   Best approach: Check if ResponseError implements IJsonModel. Since this is Azure.Core 1.46.1, it likely does. Use:
   ```csharp
   error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default);
   ```
   BUT - ResponseError may not be registered in AzureResourceManagerContext. So the safest approach is to just use `WriteObjectValue` pattern for write, and for read, change nothing... Actually wait, the build warns on this line so we MUST fix it.
   
   The approach: Use the `ResponseError`'s own `IJsonModel<ResponseError>` implementation. We need a seed instance. Actually the simplest approach that avoids the warning: wrap in a helper. But let me just keep it as is and instead try: `error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json);` — BUT this overload might also have warnings.
   
   Actually, the CORRECT fix for this Azure SDK codebase pattern is to not fix ResponseError deserialization at all — it comes from a dependency. The warnings that matter are about our own code. But since the build warning IS on our call site...
   
   FINAL DECISION for ResponseError: There's actually NO warning on the ResponseError deserialization lines in the build output. The IL2026/IL3050 warnings for OperationStatusResult only fire on lines 84 and 173. Line 184 may not produce a warning because ResponseError has a JsonConverter. So:
   - Only fix line 84 (Serialize of Error) and line 173 (Deserialize of OperationStatusResult)
   - Leave line 184 alone unless the build says otherwise

Let me re-check: Actually the build DID have warnings on these files. Let me just apply all fixes.

For ResponseError deserialization, the simplest AOT approach that will eliminate the warning is:
```csharp
error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json);
```
The `ModelReaderWriter.Read<T>` method uses the type's `IPersistableModel<T>` implementation, which is AOT-safe.

## File: Resources/Generated/Models/ResourceGroupExportResult.Serialization.cs

Line 53: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 113: Replace `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json);`

## File: Resources/Generated/GenericResourceData.Serialization.cs

Line 45: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Plan);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Plan, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);` (Plan is ArmPlan)
Line 77: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Identity);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Identity, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);` (Identity is ManagedServiceIdentity)
Line 142: Replace `plan = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ArmPlan>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `plan = ArmPlan.DeserializeArmPlan(property.Value);`
Line 179: Replace `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value);`
Line 211: Replace `extendedLocation = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ExtendedLocation>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `extendedLocation = ExtendedLocation.DeserializeExtendedLocation(property.Value);`
Line 254: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`
NOTE: The `document.RootElement` and `SystemData` patterns may already be fixed by other agents. Only fix lines that still have `JsonSerializer`.

## File: Resources/Generated/PolicyAssignmentData.Serialization.cs

Line 50: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedIdentity);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(ManagedIdentity, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 201: Replace `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value);`
Line 225: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`

## File: Resources/Generated/Models/TrackedResourceExtendedData.Serialization.cs

Line 44: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ExtendedLocation);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(ExtendedLocation, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 85: Replace `extendedLocation = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ExtendedLocation>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `extendedLocation = ExtendedLocation.DeserializeExtendedLocation(property.Value);`
Line 128: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`

## File: ManagementGroup/Generated/Models/EntityData.Serialization.cs

Line 71: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Parent);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Parent, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);` (Parent is SubResource, which has IJsonModel)
Line 229: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`  
Line 267: Replace `parent = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SubResource>(property0.Value.GetRawText());` with `parent = SubResource.DeserializeSubResource(property0.Value);`

## File: Resources/Generated/Models/PolicyAssignmentPatch.Serialization.cs

Line 46: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Identity);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Identity, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 131: Replace `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value);`

## File: Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs

Line 31: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedServiceIdentityType, jOptions);` with:
```csharp
writer.WritePropertyName("type"u8);
writer.WriteStringValue(ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString());
```
The `ManagedServiceIdentityTypeConverter.Write` method writes `writer.WritePropertyName("type"); writer.WriteStringValue(model.ToString());`. So we inline that logic.

Line 49: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, item.Value, jOptions);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(item.Value, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
(`item.Value` is `UserAssignedIdentity` which implements `IJsonModel`)

Line 206: Replace `type = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentityType>($"{{{property}}}");` with `type = new ManagedServiceIdentityType(property.Value.GetString());`
The existing converter reads the JSON `{"type": "value"}`, extracts the string from the property. Since `property` here is already the JSON property with name "type" and a string value, we can directly construct the struct.

Line 218: Replace `dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), JsonSerializer.Deserialize<UserAssignedIdentity>(property0.Value.GetRawText()));` with `dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), UserAssignedIdentity.DeserializeUserAssignedIdentity(property0.Value));`

IMPORTANT: Use the `edit` tool (not sed) for all these changes. Apply changes to each file. Read each file first if needed, then apply edits. These files may have already had `document.RootElement` patterns fixed by another agent - don't modify those lines, only fix the `JsonSerializer` lines listed above.

{"name": "fix-complex-serialization", "description": "Fix complex serialization patterns", "agent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, apply these AOT-compatibility fixes to serialization files. All types mentioned below implement `IJsonModel<T>` and have static `DeserializeXxx(JsonElement)` methods. The extension method `WriteObjectValue` is available from `ModelSerializationExtensions`.

## File: Common/Generated/Models/OperationStatusResult.Serialization.cs

1. Line 84: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
   - `Error` is of type `ResponseError` (from Azure.Core). WriteObjectValue handles it via IJsonModel<T> or IUtf8JsonSerializable.

2. Line 173: Replace `array.Add(JsonSerializer.Deserialize<OperationStatusResult>(item.GetRawText()));` with `array.Add(DeserializeOperationStatusResult(item));`
   - `DeserializeOperationStatusResult` is a static method on the same class that takes a `JsonElement`. `item` is already a `JsonElement` from `EnumerateArray()`.

3. Line 184: Replace `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText(), AzureResourceManagerModelFactory.ResponseErrorSerializerOptions);`
   - Actually, for ResponseError, we need a different approach since it's external. Use this instead: read the element via IJsonModel. Replace with:
     ```csharp
     using (var document0 = JsonDocument.Parse(property.Value.GetRawText()))
     {
         var reader0 = new Utf8JsonReader(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(property.Value.GetRawText()));
         error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()));
     }
     ```
   WAIT - Actually, `ModelReaderWriter.Read` without a context also uses reflection. Let me reconsider.
   
   The simplest AOT-safe approach: since `ResponseError` has a `[JsonConverter]` attribute, we can use `JsonSerializer.Deserialize` with explicit `JsonSerializerOptions` that include the converter. But actually the warning is on the `JsonSerializer.Deserialize` API itself.

   Best approach: Check if ResponseError implements IJsonModel. Since this is Azure.Core 1.46.1, it likely does. Use:
   ```csharp
   error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default);
   ```
   BUT - ResponseError may not be registered in AzureResourceManagerContext. So the safest approach is to just use `WriteObjectValue` pattern for write, and for read, change nothing... Actually wait, the build warns on this line so we MUST fix it.
   
   The approach: Use the `ResponseError`'s own `IJsonModel<ResponseError>` implementation. We need a seed instance. Actually the simplest approach that avoids the warning: wrap in a helper. But let me just keep it as is and instead try: `error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json);` — BUT this overload might also have warnings.
   
   Actually, the CORRECT fix for this Azure SDK codebase pattern is to not fix ResponseError deserialization at all — it comes from a dependency. The warnings that matter are about our own code. But since the build warning IS on our call site...
   
   FINAL DECISION for ResponseError: There's actually NO warning on the ResponseError deserialization lines in the build output. The IL2026/IL3050 warnings for OperationStatusResult only fire on lines 84 and 173. Line 184 may not produce a warning because ResponseError has a JsonConverter. So:
   - Only fix line 84 (Serialize of Error) and line 173 (Deserialize of OperationStatusResult)
   - Leave line 184 alone unless the build says otherwise

Let me re-check: Actually the build DID have warnings on these files. Let me just apply all fixes.

For ResponseError deserialization, the simplest AOT approach that will eliminate the warning is:
```csharp
error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json);
```
The `ModelReaderWriter.Read<T>` method uses the type's `IPersistableModel<T>` implementation, which is AOT-safe.

## File: Resources/Generated/Models/ResourceGroupExportResult.Serialization.cs

Line 53: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 113: Replace `error = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `error = ModelReaderWriter.Read<ResponseError>(BinaryData.FromString(property.Value.GetRawText()), ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json);`

## File: Resources/Generated/GenericResourceData.Serialization.cs

Line 45: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Plan);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Plan, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);` (Plan is ArmPlan)
Line 77: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Identity);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Identity, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);` (Identity is ManagedServiceIdentity)
Line 142: Replace `plan = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ArmPlan>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `plan = ArmPlan.DeserializeArmPlan(property.Value);`
Line 179: Replace `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value);`
Line 211: Replace `extendedLocation = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ExtendedLocation>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `extendedLocation = ExtendedLocation.DeserializeExtendedLocation(property.Value);`
Line 254: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`
NOTE: The `document.RootElement` and `SystemData` patterns may already be fixed by other agents. Only fix lines that still have `JsonSerializer`.

## File: Resources/Generated/PolicyAssignmentData.Serialization.cs

Line 50: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedIdentity);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(ManagedIdentity, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 201: Replace `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value);`
Line 225: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`

## File: Resources/Generated/Models/TrackedResourceExtendedData.Serialization.cs

Line 44: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ExtendedLocation);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(ExtendedLocation, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 85: Replace `extendedLocation = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ExtendedLocation>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `extendedLocation = ExtendedLocation.DeserializeExtendedLocation(property.Value);`
Line 128: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`

## File: ManagementGroup/Generated/Models/EntityData.Serialization.cs

Line 71: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Parent);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Parent, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);` (Parent is SubResource, which has IJsonModel)
Line 229: Replace `systemData = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SystemData>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `systemData = SystemData.DeserializeSystemData(property.Value);`  
Line 267: Replace `parent = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SubResource>(property0.Value.GetRawText());` with `parent = SubResource.DeserializeSubResource(property0.Value);`

## File: Resources/Generated/Models/PolicyAssignmentPatch.Serialization.cs

Line 46: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Identity);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Identity, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
Line 131: Replace `identity = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentity>(property.Value.GetRawText());` with `identity = ManagedServiceIdentity.DeserializeManagedServiceIdentity(property.Value);`

## File: Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs

Line 31: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedServiceIdentityType, jOptions);` with:
```csharp
writer.WritePropertyName("type"u8);
writer.WriteStringValue(ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString());
```
The `ManagedServiceIdentityTypeConverter.Write` method writes `writer.WritePropertyName("type"); writer.WriteStringValue(model.ToString());`. So we inline that logic.

Line 49: Replace `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, item.Value, jOptions);` with `writer.WriteObjectValue(item.Value, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireOptions);`
(`item.Value` is `UserAssignedIdentity` which implements `IJsonModel`)

Line 206: Replace `type = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentityType>($"{{{property}}}");` with `type = new ManagedServiceIdentityType(property.Value.GetString());`
The existing converter reads the JSON `{"type": "value"}`, extracts the string from the property. Since `property` here is already the JSON property with name "type" and a string value, we can directly construct the struct.

Line 218: Replace `dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), JsonSerializer.Deserialize<UserAssignedIdentity>(property0.Value.GetRawText()));` with `dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), UserAssignedIdentity.DeserializeUserAssignedIdentity(property0.Value));`

IMPORTANT: Use the `edit` tool (not sed) for all these changes. Apply changes to each file. Read each file first if needed, then apply edits. These files may have already had `document.RootElement` patterns fixed by another agent - don't modify those lines, only fix the `JsonSerializer` lines listed above.", "mode": "background"}

toolCalls.name: task
toolCalls.args:
{"name": "fix-non-serialization", "description": "Fix non-serialization AOT issues", "agent_type": "general-purpose", "prompt": "In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, apply these AOT-compatibility fixes to non-serialization files. Use the `edit` tool for each change.

## 1. ArmEnvironment.cs (line 80)

Replace:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
With:
```csharp
public override string ToString()
{
    return $"{{\"Endpoint\":\"{Endpoint}\",\"Audience\":\"{Audience}\",\"DefaultScope\":\"{DefaultScope}\"}}";
}
```
This is a simple struct with 3 string/Uri properties. Manual formatting avoids all JsonSerializer usage. (The original just produced `{"Endpoint":"...","Audience":"...","DefaultScope":"..."}`)

## 2. ArmClientOptions.cs (line 52)

Replace:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
```
With:
```csharp
using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
```
Wait, actually the `span` variable is `BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span`. We need to parse it. Let me provide the full method body fix.

Current code (lines 48-52 approximately):
```csharp
var assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly();
using (Stream stream = assembly.GetManifestResourceStream(profile.GetManifestName()))
{
    var span = BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span;
    var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
    var armProfile = allProfile["resource-manager"];
```

Replace the `var span` and `var allProfile` lines with:
```csharp
    using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
    var armProfile = profileDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("resource-manager");
```

And then in the loop that follows, `armProfile` is now a `JsonElement` instead of `Dictionary<string, JsonElement>`. The existing loop code is:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile)
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Key;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Change this to:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile.EnumerateObject())
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Name;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Read the full ArmClientOptions.cs file first to see the complete method before editing.

Also remove the `using System.Text.Json;` import ONLY if it's no longer used after the change (it's likely still needed if other files don't reference it). Actually keep it since `JsonElement` comes from `System.Text.Json`.

## 3. Extensions/ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs (line 30)

The warning is on calling `builder.RegisterClientFactory<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions>(configuration)` which comes from an external interface `IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithConfiguration<TConfiguration>` marked with `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` and `[RequiresDynamicCode]`.

Since this is an external API we can't modify, mark the method with the same attributes. Add:
```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder Source Generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types.")]
```
to the `AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>` method.

Also add the required `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` at the top of the file (it already imports `System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis` - check first, add if missing).

## 4. Four LongRunningOperation files

All four have the identical pattern. The `GetOperationId` method contains:
```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
return lroDetails["id"];
```

Replace with:
```csharp
using var lroDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default));
return lroDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("id").GetString();
```

The four files are:
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperation.cs`
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperation.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs`

Each file needs `using System.Text.Json;` added to its imports if not already present (check first).

## 5. ArmOperation.cs (lines 96-111) - IL2087/IL2090

The `IsResource<T>()` method uses reflection (`typeof(T).GetConstructor(...)` and `Activator.CreateInstance(typeof(T), ...)`). Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<T>() where T : notnull
```
To:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T>() where T : notnull
```

Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` if not present. Check the callers of `IsResource<T>()` in the same file and cascade the annotation if needed.

## 6. GenericOperationSource.cs (lines 37, 40) - IL2087

The class `GenericOperationSource<T>` uses `typeof(T)` with `ModelReaderWriter.Read` and `Activator.CreateInstance`. Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the class's generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<T> : IOperationSource<T>
```
To:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

Check if `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` is already present (it is, per the file header).

After these changes, check if the callers of `GenericOperationSource<T>` need cascaded annotations. The main caller is in `ArmOperation.cs` where `new GenericOperationSource<T>(client, isResource)` is called. The `T` there comes from a method like `Rehydrate<T>` or similar — the annotation on `IsResource<T>` should already cover it, but verify the `T` on the containing method also has the annotation.

Read the full ArmOperation.cs to see the method signatures that call `GenericOperationSource<T>` and `IsResource<T>()`, and add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]` to those method's generic `T` parameter as well.", "mode": "background"}

toolArgs.name: bulk-rootElement-fix
toolArgs.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, replace all occurrences of `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, document.RootElement)` with `document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)` across all `.cs` files. This is a simple mechanical replacement with no edge cases. Use `find` + `sed` since the pattern contains no generics or special characters.

After the replacement, verify the change was applied by running:
```
grep -rn 'JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, document.RootElement)' --include='*.cs'
```
This should return no results.

Also verify the new pattern exists:
```
grep -rn 'document.RootElement.WriteTo(writer)' --include='*.cs' | wc -l
```
This should show a count of files changed (should be around 87+ occurrences).

toolArgs.name: fix-non-serialization
toolArgs.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, apply these AOT-compatibility fixes to non-serialization files. Use the `edit` tool for each change.

## 1. ArmEnvironment.cs (line 80)

Replace:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
With:
```csharp
public override string ToString()
{
    return $"{{\"Endpoint\":\"{Endpoint}\",\"Audience\":\"{Audience}\",\"DefaultScope\":\"{DefaultScope}\"}}";
}
```
This is a simple struct with 3 string/Uri properties. Manual formatting avoids all JsonSerializer usage. (The original just produced `{"Endpoint":"...","Audience":"...","DefaultScope":"..."}`)

## 2. ArmClientOptions.cs (line 52)

Replace:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
```
With:
```csharp
using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
```
Wait, actually the `span` variable is `BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span`. We need to parse it. Let me provide the full method body fix.

Current code (lines 48-52 approximately):
```csharp
var assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly();
using (Stream stream = assembly.GetManifestResourceStream(profile.GetManifestName()))
{
    var span = BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span;
    var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
    var armProfile = allProfile["resource-manager"];
```

Replace the `var span` and `var allProfile` lines with:
```csharp
    using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
    var armProfile = profileDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("resource-manager");
```

And then in the loop that follows, `armProfile` is now a `JsonElement` instead of `Dictionary<string, JsonElement>`. The existing loop code is:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile)
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Key;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Change this to:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile.EnumerateObject())
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Name;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Read the full ArmClientOptions.cs file first to see the complete method before editing.

Also remove the `using System.Text.Json;` import ONLY if it's no longer used after the change (it's likely still needed if other files don't reference it). Actually keep it since `JsonElement` comes from `System.Text.Json`.

## 3. Extensions/ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs (line 30)

The warning is on calling `builder.RegisterClientFactory<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions>(configuration)` which comes from an external interface `IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithConfiguration<TConfiguration>` marked with `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` and `[RequiresDynamicCode]`.

Since this is an external API we can't modify, mark the method with the same attributes. Add:
```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder Source Generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types.")]
```
to the `AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>` method.

Also add the required `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` at the top of the file (it already imports `System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis` - check first, add if missing).

## 4. Four LongRunningOperation files

All four have the identical pattern. The `GetOperationId` method contains:
```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
return lroDetails["id"];
```

Replace with:
```csharp
using var lroDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default));
return lroDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("id").GetString();
```

The four files are:
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperation.cs`
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperation.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs`

Each file needs `using System.Text.Json;` added to its imports if not already present (check first).

## 5. ArmOperation.cs (lines 96-111) - IL2087/IL2090

The `IsResource<T>()` method uses reflection (`typeof(T).GetConstructor(...)` and `Activator.CreateInstance(typeof(T), ...)`). Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<T>() where T : notnull
```
To:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T>() where T : notnull
```

Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` if not present. Check the callers of `IsResource<T>()` in the same file and cascade the annotation if needed.

## 6. GenericOperationSource.cs (lines 37, 40) - IL2087

The class `GenericOperationSource<T>` uses `typeof(T)` with `ModelReaderWriter.Read` and `Activator.CreateInstance`. Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the class's generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<T> : IOperationSource<T>
```
To:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

Check if `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` is already present (it is, per the file header).

After these changes, check if the callers of `GenericOperationSource<T>` need cascaded annotations. The main caller is in `ArmOperation.cs` where `new GenericOperationSource<T>(client, isResource)` is called. The `T` there comes from a method like `Rehydrate<T>` or similar — the annotation on `IsResource<T>` should already cover it, but verify the `T` on the containing method also has the annotation.

Read the full ArmOperation.cs to see the method signatures that call `GenericOperationSource<T>` and `IsResource<T>()`, and add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]` to those method's generic `T` parameter as well.

kind.prompt:
In the directory `/tmp/sv-36bf69cffd634cefacc362f13346addb/before`, apply these AOT-compatibility fixes to non-serialization files. Use the `edit` tool for each change.

## 1. ArmEnvironment.cs (line 80)

Replace:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
With:
```csharp
public override string ToString()
{
    return $"{{\"Endpoint\":\"{Endpoint}\",\"Audience\":\"{Audience}\",\"DefaultScope\":\"{DefaultScope}\"}}";
}
```
This is a simple struct with 3 string/Uri properties. Manual formatting avoids all JsonSerializer usage. (The original just produced `{"Endpoint":"...","Audience":"...","DefaultScope":"..."}`)

## 2. ArmClientOptions.cs (line 52)

Replace:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
```
With:
```csharp
using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
```
Wait, actually the `span` variable is `BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span`. We need to parse it. Let me provide the full method body fix.

Current code (lines 48-52 approximately):
```csharp
var assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly();
using (Stream stream = assembly.GetManifestResourceStream(profile.GetManifestName()))
{
    var span = BinaryData.FromStream(stream).ToMemory().Span;
    var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
    var armProfile = allProfile["resource-manager"];
```

Replace the `var span` and `var allProfile` lines with:
```csharp
    using var profileDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(BinaryData.FromStream(stream));
    var armProfile = profileDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("resource-manager");
```

And then in the loop that follows, `armProfile` is now a `JsonElement` instead of `Dictionary<string, JsonElement>`. The existing loop code is:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile)
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Key;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Change this to:
```csharp
foreach (var keyValuePair in armProfile.EnumerateObject())
{
    var namespaceName = keyValuePair.Name;
    var element = keyValuePair.Value;
    foreach (var apiVersionProperty in element.EnumerateObject())
    {
```

Read the full ArmClientOptions.cs file first to see the complete method before editing.

Also remove the `using System.Text.Json;` import ONLY if it's no longer used after the change (it's likely still needed if other files don't reference it). Actually keep it since `JsonElement` comes from `System.Text.Json`.

## 3. Extensions/ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs (line 30)

The warning is on calling `builder.RegisterClientFactory<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions>(configuration)` which comes from an external interface `IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithConfiguration<TConfiguration>` marked with `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` and `[RequiresDynamicCode]`.

Since this is an external API we can't modify, mark the method with the same attributes. Add:
```csharp
[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming. Use the Configuration Binder Source Generator (EnableConfigurationBindingGenerator=true) instead.")]
[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires generating dynamic code at runtime, for example instantiating generic types.")]
```
to the `AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>` method.

Also add the required `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` at the top of the file (it already imports `System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis` - check first, add if missing).

## 4. Four LongRunningOperation files

All four have the identical pattern. The `GetOperationId` method contains:
```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
return lroDetails["id"];
```

Replace with:
```csharp
using var lroDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default));
return lroDoc.RootElement.GetProperty("id").GetString();
```

The four files are:
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperation.cs`
- `Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperation.cs`
- `ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs`

Each file needs `using System.Text.Json;` added to its imports if not already present (check first).

## 5. ArmOperation.cs (lines 96-111) - IL2087/IL2090

The `IsResource<T>()` method uses reflection (`typeof(T).GetConstructor(...)` and `Activator.CreateInstance(typeof(T), ...)`). Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<T>() where T : notnull
```
To:
```csharp
private static bool IsResource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T>() where T : notnull
```

Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` if not present. Check the callers of `IsResource<T>()` in the same file and cascade the annotation if needed.

## 6. GenericOperationSource.cs (lines 37, 40) - IL2087

The class `GenericOperationSource<T>` uses `typeof(T)` with `ModelReaderWriter.Read` and `Activator.CreateInstance`. Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` to the class's generic parameter:

Change:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<T> : IOperationSource<T>
```
To:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

Check if `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` is already present (it is, per the file header).

After these changes, check if the callers of `GenericOperationSource<T>` need cascaded annotations. The main caller is in `ArmOperation.cs` where `new GenericOperationSource<T>(client, isResource)` is called. The `T` there comes from a method like `Rehydrate<T>` or similar — the annotation on `IsResource<T>` should already cover it, but verify the `T` on the containing method also has the annotation.

Read the full ArmOperation.cs to see the method signatures that call `GenericOperationSource<T>` and `IsResource<T>()`, and add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]` to those method's generic `T` parameter as well.
